notes:
Game runs at 60fps, sound seems fine, no missing textures etc. However if I start the game with my Wireless 360 Controller connected it gets picked up as a Playstation one with an incorrect mapping. I have to launch the game with controller disconnected, go into settings, connect the controller and then switch from mouse -> controller for it to work.

I can't compare this to Windows, so if the issue exists there too then please consider this a "Platinum" rating.
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes: After playing more than 60+ hours using Proton, this game run flawlessly, but ... There is an annoying bug: If you switch off your controller and switch back on, the game will be stuck on the message which looks like "Waiting for a controller, press A key for continue...".
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes: The game run flawlessly at max settings and 1440p. That said, my xbox controller gets detected as a playstation controller when the game start. I can't even get to the settings to change the controller layout. In order to fix this, I start the game only with my keyboard. Then I go the the controller settings, I plug my xbox controller and change the controller layout to xbox.
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes: Runs perfectly fine. Even reaches 60fps at 1440p with low settings. 60fps 1080p with higher settings.
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes:
Completed the entire game with Proton from start to finish. Runs at stable 144 FPS at 1440p with all graphical options set to maximum except anti-aliasing.

No graphical issues as far as I can tell.

The game has a few minor issues: the game is finicky with the DualShock 4 controller: if you unplug it and plug it again during gameplay, it will act as if one of the buttons was stuck. There were also rare issues with audio playback (an ambient audio track seems to continue looping for longer when it shouldn't). These issues could also happen on Windows, but I haven't checked.

The game also has post-processing blur that looks ugly at times: the blur could be disabled with a 3rd party mod bundled with Special K. Unfortunately, that mod doesn't work with Proton.
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes: Works out of the box on Ubuntu MATE 18.04.2 LTS and Logitech F710 works properly. Total play time of 30 minutes. Game play video here --> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uVPOqo3wOA0
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes: Full playthrough with Steam controller (72 hours) at 1440p high. Everything ran perfectly, and the game didn't freeze at all (my previous report was borked due to the game freezing right at the beginning).

notes: An amazing game that just works perfectly on proton. I played with a steam controller and the game at max settings at 1440p 144Hz. The fps dropped off in a few areas, but never dipped below 60 (I had a similar problem on Windows). As far as  I am aware the mod SpecialK does not work, but the game seemed to run just fine.
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition

notes: No tweaks necessary; everything worked out of the box. Performance wasn't great, but this was a test for the Intel iGPU rather than dedicated graphics - maxing out graphics options at 720p (fullscreen/vsync) still netted 30-45 FPS in fields and 60ish in most battles.
title: Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition
